serviceDescription: With Amazon Data firehose, you pay for the volume of data you ingest into the service. There are no set up fees or upfront commitments. You are billed for the volume of data ingested into Amazon Data Firehose, and if applicable, for data format conversion to Apache Parquet or ORC.

cards.title: Data Ingestion
cards.helpText: Pricing is based on the volume of data ingested into Amazon Data firehose, which is calculated as the number of data records you send to the service, times the size of each record rounded up to the nearest 5KB. For example, if your data records are 42KB each, Amazon Data Firehose will count each record as 45KB of data ingested. Usage charges associated with Amazon S3, Amazon Redshift, Amazon Elasticsearch Service, and Splunk are billed separately. Data transfer usage charges for cross-region and to the internet are billed separately.

components.label: Record size
components.description: Size of each record. If source type is Direct PUT or Kinesis Data Stream, records are rounded up to the nearest 5 KB. For example, a 42 KB record is counted as 45 KB. If source type is Vended Logs, records are NOT rounded up.

exists.meteredUnit: Per GB of data format conversion

cards.title: Data Format Conversion
cards.helpText: Pricing is based on the volume of data ingested into Amazon Data firehose, which is calculated as the number of data records you send to the service, times the size of each record rounded up to the nearest 5KB. For example, if your data records are 42KB each, Amazon Data Firehose will count each record as 45KB of data ingested. If you configure your delivery stream to convert the incoming data into Apache Parquet or Apache ORC format before the data is delivered to destinations, format conversion charges apply based on the volume of the incoming data.

components.text: If you configure your delivery stream to convert the incoming data into Apache Parquet or Apache ORC format before the data is delivered to destinations, additional data format conversion charges apply based on the volume of the incoming data.

exists.meteredUnit: Per GB of dynamically partitioned data delivered

cards.title: Dynamic Partitioning
cards.helpText: You can enable Dynamic Partitioning to continuously group data by keys in your records (such as “customer_id”), and have data delivered to S3 prefixes mapped to each key. With Dynamic Partitioning, you pay per GB processed through Dynamic Partitioning, per object, and optionally per JQ processing hour for data parsing.

components.text: To estimate the cost of Dynamic Partitioning, please ensure to enter a value to the 'Number of records for Data Ingestion' field in Data Ingestion card above.

cards.title: Data Processed to VPC
cards.helpText: Pricing is based on the volume of data ingested into Amazon Data firehose, which is calculated as the number of data records you send to the service, times the size of each record rounded up to the nearest 5KB. For example, if your data records are 42KB each, Amazon Data Firehose will count each record as 45KB of data ingested. If you configure your delivery stream to deliver to a destination that resides in a VPC, you will be charged based on the volume of data processed via the VPC and for the number of hours that your delivery stream is active in each subnet.

components.text: If you configure your delivery stream to deliver to a destination that resides in a VPC, you will be charged based on the volume of data processed via the VPC and for the number of hours that your delivery stream is active in each subnet.

components.description: In cases where data delivery to destinations fails, Amazon Data Firehose retries sending the data based on the back off algorithm and the retry duration you configured in the delivery stream.
